messages.user_message: Tell me about your poker background and any AI applications in poker
messages.bot_response:
I played poker professionally from 2018 to 2021, and it was quite an experience! During my poker career, I relied heavily on AI-driven tools known as "solvers." These programs use complex algorithms to analyze game scenarios and help players make optimal decisions. The most common method used by these solvers is Counterfactual Regret Minimization (CFR), which is an AI technique that calculates the best possible moves in a poker game by minimizing regret over time.

In addition to my personal poker journey, I also ventured into the business side of poker by attempting to build an online business called GTO Inspector. This was aimed at the Indian poker ecosystem, leveraging my poker experience and insights.

Eventually, I decided to leave the full-time poker scene despite its profitability. The decision was driven by various personal and professional reasons, including the desire to explore other opportunities.

AI's application in poker is fascinating because it pushes the boundaries of game theory and decision-making, providing insights that are not only applicable in poker but also in areas like finance and strategic business planning.
